workflow_name: Customer Renewal Email -> Forecast Impact + Path
user_message: Just came out of escalation triage with Adatum Corporation. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Nguyen <laura.nguyen@adatumcorpor.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-02 10:54 EST
Subject: Follow-up: escalation triage Review

Just checking in on the escalation triage proposal. Our Infrastructure Manager is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Nguyen <laura.nguyen@adatumcorpor.com>
Date: 2024-02-16 09:12 EST
Subject: URGENT: escalation triage Issue at Adatum Corporation

Our team encountered some challenges with escalation triage. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Adatum Corporation: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

required_artifacts: path forward with MACC/funding options if applicable

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF35-M trap (Separation of renewal handling across 4 skills means CSA gets fragmented narrati); produces complete Contract state summary with freshness, forecast impact with confidence, response draft (no commercia
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF35-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF35-M failure mode (Separation of renewal handling across 4 skills means CSA gets fragmented narrati)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF35-M -- Separation of renewal handling across 4 skills means CSA gets fragmented narrati
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
